data_version.rule: Versioned D1 dataset identifier; calendar semantic changes require a new data_version before formal use.

universe_id.rule: Research universe identifier; D1-T02 does not materialize universe membership.

time_segment_id.rule: Configured time segment key; calendar rows must not imply dates outside the approved segment boundary.

exchange.rule: Canonical exchange code from the project enum: SSE or SZSE.

trading_date.rule: Exchange-local calendar date in Asia/Shanghai semantics; not a timestamp and not a data retrieval date.

is_trading_day.rule: True only when the exchange session is open for normal trading under the source calendar evidence; unknown must not be converted to false.

calendar_session_type.rule: Controlled session classification for the exchange date.

source_registry_id.rule: Registry id of the approved source snapshot; formal ingestion remains unauthorized in D1-T02.

source_snapshot_id.rule: Immutable raw snapshot reference required before any future loader can write formal rows.

run_id.rule: Run manifest id required before formal materialization; D1-T02 does not create run manifests.

exchange_policy.exchange_code_system: Use project canonical exchange codes: SSE for Shanghai Stock Exchange and SZSE for Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

calendar_session_type.unknown_value_rule: unknown may be used only when source quality is explicitly unresolved and must block formal downstream release.

source_registry_requirements.reason: HiThink remains only a candidate source for trading_calendar; source terms, as-of behavior, revision behavior, raw snapshot retention, manifests, and run authorization are not closed.

tables.as_of_policy: Calendar facts must use retrieval observed_at and any exchange-declared effective dates; formal use is blocked until these semantics are reviewed.
tables.revision_policy: Calendar revisions require a new source_snapshot_id or data_version and a diff attribution record; old snapshots remain immutable.
tables.downstream_boundary: Trading calendar rows must not be read directly by R0; R0 can use calendar facts only through d3.daily_market_observations references.

data_version.rule: Versioned D1 dataset identifier; trading constraint semantic changes require a new data_version before formal use.

universe_id.rule: Research universe identifier; D1-T02 does not materialize universe membership.

time_segment_id.rule: Configured time segment key; constraint rows must not imply dates outside the approved segment boundary.

security_id.rule: Project-internal stable security key from the D1-T01 contract.

trading_date.rule: Exchange-local trading or calendar date in Asia/Shanghai semantics.

is_suspended.rule: Suspension fact for the security/date; unknown source quality must not be converted to false.

is_st.rule: ST or special-treatment fact for the security/date; this is a trading constraint fact, not a security_type.

limit_up_price.rule: Raw exchange limit-up price when applicable; null is allowed only when no applicable limit exists or the approved source explicitly reports unknown.

limit_down_price.rule: Raw exchange limit-down price when applicable; null is allowed only when no applicable limit exists or the approved source explicitly reports unknown.

price_limit_status.rule: Controlled price-limit status for the security/date; unknown must remain explicit.

tradable_flag.rule: Boolean tradability fact after applying suspension and source-declared constraints; unknown must not be silently converted to false.

source_registry_id.rule: Registry id of the approved source snapshot; formal ingestion remains unauthorized in D1-T02.

source_snapshot_id.rule: Immutable raw snapshot reference required before any future loader can write formal rows.

observed_at.rule: Timestamp at which the constraint fact was observed or retrieved; future loaders must enforce observed_at-bounded use.

run_id.rule: Run manifest id required before formal materialization; D1-T02 does not create run manifests.

exchange_policy.exchange_code_system: Trading constraints inherit canonical exchange semantics through security_id and upstream security mapping.

price_limit_status.unknown_value_rule: unknown must remain explicit and blocks formal downstream release until quality review resolves it. Near-limit concepts belong to later D2/R or execution-quality derived layers, not D1 raw trading constraint facts.

source_registry_requirements.reason: HiThink remains a candidate source and BaoStock remains only a candidate or backup for trading_constraints; source terms, as-of behavior, revision behavior, raw snapshot retention, manifests, and run authorization are not closed.

tables.as_of_policy: Constraint facts must be observed_at-bounded and aligned to the same security_id/trading_date as raw trading facts before downstream use.
tables.revision_policy: Revised suspension, ST, tradability, or price-limit facts require a new source_snapshot_id or data_version and comparison report.
tables.downstream_boundary: Trading status, tradability, suspension, ST, and price-limit facts are execution/constraint facts, not price-shape signals; R0 may use them only through d3.daily_market_observations references.

shared_boundary.d3_entry_rule: R0 must not read d1.trading_calendar or d1.trading_constraints directly; D3 remains the only formal daily research entry point.
shared_boundary.not_price_shape_signal_rule: Trading status, tradability, suspension, ST, and price-limit facts are trading constraints rather than price compression, trend, or release signals.
shared_boundary.unknown_state_rule: unknown, missing, or low-quality status must stay explicit and must not be converted to false.

future_loader_requirements: Future loader must read only approved raw snapshots and manifests, never live APIs.

Future loader must fail non-zero on duplicate primary keys, invalid exchange, invalid controlled vocabulary values, invalid nullable fields, date/timestamp quality failures, or missing source snapshot references.
Future loader must not populate d1.trading_calendar or d1.trading_constraints until source terms, raw snapshot, manifest, as-of, revision, and run authorization gates pass.
Future loader must preserve source provenance outside DuckDB raw byte storage and must not store vendor payloads in formal tables.
